acceptance_criteria.description: Given a JSONL trace store, compute per-session retry_rate (retried_mqos / total_mqos), empty_result_rate (empty_result_mqos / total_mqos), and bind_failure_rate (failed_binds / total_mqos); return a SessionQuality struct for each session_id present.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac1.rs — fixture trace with 3 sessions of known counts; assert computed rates match expected f64 within 1e-9.

acceptance_criteria.description: Given a JSONL trace store, compute per-entity (model entity name) distributions: first_attempt_bind_rate, retry_histogram (vec of retry counts), result_fill_rate; return an EntityQuality map keyed by entity name.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac2.rs — fixture trace referencing 2 named entities; assert per-entity stats match expected values.

acceptance_criteria.description: Reading from a file path that does not exist returns Err with a descriptive IoError variant; the library never panics.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac3.rs — call score_trace_store with a nonexistent path; assert Err and no panic via std::panic::catch_unwind.

acceptance_criteria.description: A JSONL trace store containing a malformed (non-UTF-8 or non-JSON) line returns Err(ParseError) that identifies the offending line number; valid lines before the error are not returned.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac4.rs — fixture with a bad line at position 3; assert Err carrying line_number == 3.

acceptance_criteria.description: An empty JSONL file (zero bytes) returns Ok with empty session and entity maps, not Err.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac5.rs — pass empty temp file; assert Ok with empty maps.

acceptance_criteria.description: The public API is fully documented (every pub fn, struct, and enum carries a doc comment); `cargo doc --no-deps` emits zero warnings.
Scorer accepts a BufRead trait object in addition to a file path, enabling callers to pass in-memory readers for testing or streaming use.
acceptance_criteria.test: tests/acceptance_ac7.rs — pass a Cursor<Vec<u8>> to the BufRead variant; assert same output as file-path variant on identical content.

ambiguities_resolved.question: The vision lists 'mcp-interaction-quality-scorer' as a rust-cli; the context overrides to lib. Which wins?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Context wins: target is lib. The CLI surface is deferred to a thin wrapper crate that depends on this lib, consistent with the non-goal 'does not expose a CLI binary'.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should grounding_score distribution be a histogram with fixed buckets or raw scores?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: V1: return a Vec<f64> of raw grounding_score values per entity, preserving full fidelity. Callers bin as needed. Histogram bucketing is a MAY-level future enhancement.

five_whys_trace.a: Because these per-session and per-entity signals reveal where the constrained-output guarantee is fraying in real production sessions — something neither the tiger benchmark nor the grounding eval captures, since both operate on synthetic data.
five_whys_trace.q: Why score MCP interaction quality (retry rate, empty-result rate, bind failure rate)?

five_whys_trace.a: The trace store is a raw append-log; every consumer would have to re-implement the same aggregation pass (grouping by session_id, counting retries, computing rates), producing duplicated logic and divergent definitions of 'retry_rate'.
five_whys_trace.q: Why can't the trace store records alone be consumed directly by downstream tools?

five_whys_trace.a: The gap miner uses retry_rate to weight which entity gaps are most disruptive; the fine-tune exporter uses first_attempt_bind_rate to filter clean interactions. If they compute the same concept differently, the two pipelines will disagree on which interactions are high-quality — corrupting both the gap report and the fine-tuning corpus.
five_whys_trace.q: Why does a shared definition of these rates matter?

five_whys_trace.a: Versioning: when the trace-store schema adds a field (e.g. latency_ms), fixing the scorer in one lib fixes all downstream crates at once. A duplicated inline scorer in each CLI would require coordinated changes across three separate binaries with no compile-time guarantee they stay in sync.
five_whys_trace.q: Why is a lib crate (rather than baking the scorer into each downstream CLI) the right boundary?

non_goals: Does not write or mutate the trace store — read-only consumer only.
Does not implement the gap miner, fine-tune exporter, or any downstream pipeline; those are separate crates.
Does not expose a CLI binary; this is a lib crate that CLI tools link against.
Does not do clustering, ranking, or gap report generation — that is mcp-coverage-gap-miner's responsibility.
Does not fetch traces from a remote endpoint or database — input is always a local JSONL file path or BufRead.
Does not filter or export interactions for fine-tuning — that is mcp-fine-tune-exporter's responsibility.
root_motivation: Compute per-session and per-entity quality distributions from the durable trace store (retry histograms, first-attempt bind rate, result fill rate, grounding score distribution) so that downstream tools — the gap miner and fine-tune exporter — consume structured quality signals rather than re-scanning raw JSONL records on every invocation.
scope: Parse a JSONL trace store whose records conform to the mcp-trace-store schema (session_id, timestamp, MQO, bind_result, grounding_score, execute_result shape, quality_signals).
Compute per-session quality metrics: retry_rate, empty_result_rate, bind_failure_rate.
Compute per-entity quality distributions: first_attempt_bind_rate, retry_histogram, result_fill_rate, grounding_score distribution.
Return typed Rust structs (SessionQuality, EntityQuality) suitable for serialization to JSON by callers.
Return structured errors (not panics) for missing files, malformed JSONL, and schema-mismatch records.

unfakeable_metric.harness_command: cargo test --lib 2>&1 | grep -E '^test result' | awk '{print $6}' | tr -d ';'
unfakeable_metric.name: tests_pass_count

user_persona: AtScale MCP server operator or platform engineer who feeds the scorer's output JSON into the coverage-gap-miner and fine-tune-exporter pipelines to decide where the semantic model needs patching and which interactions are clean enough to use for fine-tuning.
